title: Peace Gets an Extra 3 Weeks! Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Ceasefire
summary:
Have you ever been in a fight with a friend and then agreed to a time-out? That's kind of what's happening between Israel and Lebanon right now—but on a much bigger scale.

President Trump just announced that the two countries have agreed to extend their ceasefire (a pause in fighting) by three more weeks. The first ceasefire was only 10 days long and was about to end on Monday. But after a meeting at the White House, the ambassadors from both countries said, "Let's keep the peace a little longer."

Trump said the meeting went "very well." He even posted on social media that the U.S. will help Lebanon protect itself from a group called Hezbollah. Hezbollah is a militant group (a group that uses force) that has been fighting Israel. The Lebanese government says it wants to stop Israeli attacks, get Israeli soldiers out of Lebanon, free Lebanese prisoners, and start rebuilding homes.

But here's the tricky part: Hezbollah says it won't follow any agreements made in these talks. And even though the ceasefire is on, there have already been violations (rule-breaking) by both sides. A journalist in Lebanon was killed by an Israeli strike, and an ambulance was fired on. That made a lot of people angry.

Still, many people are hopeful. They think this extra time could lead to real peace talks. Imagine if your school had a big fight, and then everyone sat down to work it out—that's what they're trying to do. Let's hope it works!
why_it_matters: This story shows how countries can try to solve big disagreements peacefully, just like you might do with a friend.

Hezbollah has rejected the talks, and there have been violations, so the ceasefire might not hold.
The extension gives both sides time to decide next steps.
